<pr_description>
Include pod UID in the log
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] I have searched the existing issues

### Is your feature request related to a problem?

_No response_

### Describe the feature you would like

Currently the Tetragon log includes IDs like a container ID. In other observability and security tools I only have attributes like the pod UID as part of the data. So, to be able to join the 2 data sources together, I would benefit from having the pod UID as part of each tetragon log line.

### Describe your proposed solution

I'm not familiar with implementation specifics of Tetragon. Therefore, I can't really propose solutions. Roughly, when enrichment based on the K8s API responses is done, the pod UID should be included in the resulting log as well.

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct

    35	  // Start time of the container.

    38	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value pid = 5;
    39	  // If this is set true, it means that the process might have been originated from
    40	  // a Kubernetes exec probe. For this field to be true, the following must be true:
    41	  // 1. The binary field matches the first element of the exec command list for either
    42	  //    liveness or readiness probe excluding the basename. For example, "/bin/ls"
    43	  //    and "ls" are considered a match.
    44	  // 2. The arguments field exactly matches the rest of the exec command list.
    45	  bool maybe_exec_probe = 13;
    46	  // The security context of the container

    48	}

    67	
    68	message Capabilities {
    69	  // Permitted set indicates what capabilities the process can use. This is a
    70	  // limiting superset for the effective capabilities that the thread may
    71	  // assume. It is also a limiting superset for the capabilities that may be
    72	  // added to the inheritable set by a thread without the CAP_SETPCAP in its
    73	  // effective set.
    74	  repeated CapabilitiesType permitted = 1;
    75	  // Effective set indicates what capabilities are active in a process. This
    76	  // is the set used by the kernel to perform permission checks for the
    77	  // thread.
    78	  repeated CapabilitiesType effective = 2;
    79	  // Inheritable set indicates which capabilities will be inherited by the
    80	  // current process when running as a root user.
    81	  repeated CapabilitiesType inheritable = 3;
    82	}
    83	
    84	message Namespace {
    85	  // Inode number of the namespace.
    86	  uint32 inum = 1;
    87	  // Indicates if namespace belongs to host.
    88	  bool is_host = 2;
    89	}
    90

    92	  // Hostname and NIS domain name.
    93	  Namespace uts = 1;
    94	  // System V IPC, POSIX message queues.

    96	  // Mount points.
    97	  Namespace mnt = 3;
    98	  // Process IDs.
    99	  Namespace pid = 4;
   100	  // Process IDs for children processes.
   101	  Namespace pid_for_children = 5;
   102	  // Network devices, stacks, ports, etc.
   103	  Namespace net = 6;
   104	  // Boot and monotonic clocks.

   107	  Namespace time_for_children = 8;

   110	  // User and group IDs.
   111	  Namespace user = 10;
   112	}

   114	message UserNamespace {
   115	  // Nested level of the user namespace. Init or host user namespace is at level 0.
   116	  google.protobuf.Int32Value level = 1;
   117	  // The owner user ID of the namespace
   118	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value uid = 2;
   119	  // The owner group ID of the namepace.
   120	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value gid = 3;
   121	  // The user namespace details that include the inode number of the namespace.
   122	  Namespace ns = 4;
   123	}
   124	
   125	message ProcessCredentials {
   126	  // The real user ID of the process' owner.
   127	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value uid = 1;
   128	  // The real group ID of the process' owner.
   129	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value gid = 2;
   130	  // The effective user ID used for permission checks.
   131	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value euid = 3;
   132	  // The effective group ID used for permission checks.
   133	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value egid = 4;
   134	  // The saved user ID.
   135	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value suid = 5;
   136	  // The saved group ID.
   137	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value sgid = 6;
   138	  // the filesystem user ID used for filesystem access checks. Usually equals the euid.
   139	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value fsuid = 7;
   140	  // The filesystem group ID used for filesystem access checks. Usually equals the egid.
   141	  google.protobuf.UInt32Value fsgid = 8;
   142	  // Secure management flags
   143	  repeated SecureBitsType securebits = 9;

If set to false, pod information will not be passed on mount. Default is false.\n\nThe CSI driver specifies podInfoOnMount as part of driver deployment. If true, Kubelet will pass pod information as VolumeContext in the CSI NodePublishVolume() calls. The CSI driver is responsible for parsing and validating the information passed in as VolumeContext.\n\nThe following VolumeContext will be passed if podInfoOnMount is set to true. This list might grow, but the prefix will be used. \"csi.storage.k8s.io/pod.name\": pod.Name \"csi.storage.k8s.io/pod.namespace\": pod.Namespace \"csi.storage.k8s.io/pod.uid\": string(pod.UID) \"csi.storage.k8s.io/ephemeral\": \"true\" if the volume is an ephemeral inline volume\n                                defined by a CSIVolumeSource, otherwise \"false\"\n\n\"csi.storage.k8s.io/ephemeral\" is a new feature in Kubernetes 1.16. It is only required for drivers which support both the \"Persistent\" and \"Ephemeral\" VolumeLifecycleMode. Other drivers can leave pod info disabled and/or ignore this field. As Kubernetes 1.15 doesn't support this field, drivers can only support one mode when deployed on such a cluster and the deployment determines which mode that is, for example via a command line parameter of the driver.\n\nThis field is immutable.",

     1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     2	// Copyright Authors of Tetragon
     3	
     4	package process
     5	
     6	import (
     7	        "github.com/cilium/tetragon/api/v1/tetragon"
     8	        "github.com/cilium/tetragon/pkg/filters"
     9	        "github.com/cilium/tetragon/pkg/logger"
    10	        "github.com/cilium/tetragon/pkg/metrics/watchermetrics"
    11	        "github.com/cilium/tetragon/pkg/option"
    12	        "github.com/cilium/tetragon/pkg/podhelpers"
    13	        "github.com/cilium/tetragon/pkg/watcher"
    14	
    15	        "google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb"
    16	        "google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/wrapperspb"
    17	        corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    18	)
    19	
    20	func getExecCommand(probe *corev1.Probe) []string {
    21	        if probe != nil && probe.Exec != nil {
    22	                return probe.Exec.Command
    23	        }
    24	        return nil
    25	}
    26	
    27	func getProbes(pod *corev1.Pod, containerStatus *corev1.ContainerStatus) ([]string, []string) {
    28	        for _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {
    29	                if container.Name == containerStatus.Name {
    30	                        return getExecCommand(container.LivenessProbe), getExecCommand(container.ReadinessProbe)
    31	                }
    32	        }
    33	        return nil, nil
    34	}
    35	
    36	func isContainerPrivileged(pod *corev1.Pod, container *corev1.ContainerStatus) bool {
    37	        for _, c := range pod.Spec.Containers {
    38	                if c.Name == container.Name {
    39	                        if c.SecurityContext != nil && c.SecurityContext.Privileged != nil {
    40	                                return *c.SecurityContext.Privileged
    41	                        }
    42	                        return false
    43	                }
    44	        }
    45	
    46	        for _, c := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {
    47	                if c.Name == container.Name {
    48	                        if c.SecurityContext != nil && c.SecurityContext.Privileged != nil {
    49	                                return *c.SecurityContext.Privileged
    50	                        }
    51	                        return false
    52	                }
    53	        }
    54	
    55	        for _, c := range pod.Spec.EphemeralContainers {
    56	                if c.Name == container.Name {
    57	                        if c.SecurityContext != nil && c.SecurityContext.Privileged != nil {
    58	                                return *c.SecurityContext.Privileged
    59	                        }
    60	                        return false
    61	                }
    62	        }
    63	
    64	        return false
    65	}
    66	
    67	func getPodInfo(
    68	        w watcher.PodAccessor,
    69	        containerID string,
    70	        binary string,
    71	        args string,
    72	        nspid uint32,
    73	) *tetragon.Pod {
    74	        if containerID == "" {
    75	                return nil
    76	        }
    77	        pod, container, ok := w.FindContainer(containerID)
    78	        if !ok {
    79	                watchermetrics.GetWatcherErrors(watchermetrics.K8sWatcher, watchermetrics.FailedToGetPodError).Inc()
    80	                logger.Trace(logger.GetLogger(), "failed to get pod", "container_id", containerID)
    81	                return nil
    82	        }
    83	        var startTime *timestamppb.Timestamp
    84	        livenessProbe, readinessProbe := getProbes(pod, container)
    85	        isPrivileged := isContainerPrivileged(pod, container)
    86	        maybeExecProbe := filters.MaybeExecProbe(binary, args, livenessProbe) ||
    87	                filters.MaybeExecProbe(binary, args, readinessProbe)
    88	        if container.State.Running != nil {
    89	                startTime = timestamppb.New(container.State.Running.StartedAt.Time)
    90	        }
    91	
    92	        // This is the PID inside the container. Don't set it if zero.
    93	        var containerPID *wrapperspb.UInt32Value
    94	        if nspid > 0 {
    95	                containerPID = &wrapperspb.UInt32Value{
    96	                        Value: nspid,
    97	                }
    98	        }
    99	        workloadObject, workloadType := podhelpers.GetWorkloadMetaFromPod(pod)
   100	        watchermetrics.GetWatcherEvents(watchermetrics.K8sWatcher).Inc()
   101	        podInfo := &tetragon.Pod{
   102	                Namespace:    pod.Namespace,
   103	                Workload:     workloadObject.Name,
   104	                WorkloadKind: workloadType.Kind,
   105	                Name:         pod.Name,
   106	                PodLabels:    pod.Labels,
   107	                Container: &tetragon.Container{
   108	                        Id:   container.ContainerID,
   109	                        Pid:  containerPID,
   110	                        Name: container.Name,
   111	                        Image: &tetragon.Image{
   112	                                Id:   container.ImageID,
   113	                                Name: container.Image,
   114	                        },
   115	                        StartTime:      startTime,
   116	                        MaybeExecProbe: maybeExecProbe,
   117	                        SecurityContext: &tetragon.SecurityContext{
   118	                                Privileged: isPrivileged,
   119	                        },
   120	                },
   121	        }
   122	        if option.Config.EnablePodAnnotations {
   123	                podInfo.PodAnnotations = pod.Annotations
   124	        }
   125	        return podInfo
   126	}
   127

   607	type Capabilities struct {

   609	        // Permitted set indicates what capabilities the process can use. This is a
   610	        // limiting superset for the effective capabilities that the thread may
   611	        // assume. It is also a limiting superset for the capabilities that may be
   612	        // added to the inheritable set by a thread without the CAP_SETPCAP in its
   613	        // effective set.

   620	        // current process when running as a root user.

package main

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"

    "github.com/cilium/tetragon/api/v1/tetragon"
)

func main() {
    p := &tetragon.Pod{Uid: "pod-uid-123"}
    b, err := json.Marshal(p)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    fmt.Println(p.GetUid())
    fmt.Println(string(b))
}
GOEOF

EOF

  2930	func (checker *PodChecker) Check(event *tetragon.Pod) error {

  2932	                return fmt.Errorf("%s: Pod field is nil", CheckerLogPrefix(checker))
  2933	        }
  2934

  2960	                                                }

  2963	                                }
  2964	                        }

  2969	                                        if _, ok := matched[k]; !ok {
  2970	                                                unmatched = append(unmatched, k)
  2971	                                        }
  2972	                                }
  2973	                                return fmt.Errorf("PodLabels unmatched: %v", unmatched)
  2974	                        }

  2979	                        }

   143	                w,
   144	        }
   145	}
   146	
   147	func (p *ProtojsonEncoder) Encode(v interface{}) error {
   148	        // TODO(WF): We may want to implement a streaming API here, similar to what they do in
   149	        // encoding/json. For now, I think this is probably fine though.
   150	        event, ok := v.(*tetragon.GetEventsResponse)
   151	        if !ok {
   152	                return ErrInvalidEvent
   153	        }
   154	        out, err := protojson.MarshalOptions{
   155	                // Our old exporter's behaviour was to use the snake_case names rather than
   156	                // camelCase. We want to maintain backward compatibility here so let's do the
   157	                // same thing in the protojson encoder.
   158	                UseProtoNames: true,
   159	        }.Marshal(event)
   160	        if err != nil {
   161	                return err
   162	        }
   163	        fmt.Fprintln(p.w, string(out))
   164	        return nil
   165	}

import re, ast
src=open('api/v1/tetragon/tetragon.pb.go').read()
m=re.search(r'file_tetragon_tetragon_proto_rawDesc = \[\]byte\{(.*?)\n\}', src, re.S)
body=m.group(1)
nums=[int(x,0) for x in re.findall(r'0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\b\d+\b', body)]
b=bytes(nums)
print('len',len(b), b[:20])

def read_varint(data,i):
    shift=0; res=0
    while True:
        c=data[i]; i+=1
        res |= (c&0x7f)<<shift
        if c<128: return res,i
        shift+=7

def parse(data):
    i=0; out=[]
    while i<len(data):
        key,i0=read_varint(data,i); field=key>>3; wt=key&7; i=i0
        if wt==0:
            start=i; v,i=read_varint(data,i); out.append((field,wt,data[start:i],v))
        elif wt==1:
            out.append((field,wt,data[i:i+8],None)); i+=8
        elif wt==2:
            l,i=read_varint(data,i); out.append((field,wt,data[i:i+l],None)); i+=l
        elif wt==5:
            out.append((field,wt,data[i:i+4],None)); i+=4
        else: raise Exception('wt',wt,'at',i)
    return out

fd=parse(b)
print([(f,wt,len(v)) for f,wt,v,_ in fd[:20]])
for f,wt,v,_ in fd:
    if f==4:
        msg=parse(v); name=None
        for ff,ww,vv,ival in msg:
            if ff==1: name=vv.decode()
        if name in ('Pod','Container'):
            print('message', name, 'len', len(v))
            for ff,ww,vv,ival in msg:
                if ff==2:
                    fld=parse(vv); d={}
                    for a,bw,cv,iv in fld:
                        if a in (1,6,10): d[a]=cv.decode()
                        elif a in (3,4,5): d[a]=iv
                    print(' field',d)
                elif ff==4:
                    nested=parse(vv); n=None
                    for a,bw,cv,iv in nested:
                        if a==1:n=cv.decode()
                    print(' nested',n)

cd /testbed && python3 - <<'PY'
import re
src=open('api/v1/tetragon/tetragon.pb.go').read()
body=re.search(r'file_tetragon_tetragon_proto_rawDesc = \[\]byte\{(.*?)\n\}', src, re.S).group(1)
nums=[int(x,0) for x in re.findall(r'0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\b\d+\b', body)]
b=bytes(nums)

def read_varint(data,i):
    shift=0; res=0
    while True:
        c=data[i]; i+=1
        res |= (c&0x7f)<<shift
        if c<128: return res,i
        shift+=7

def enc_varint(n):
    out=[]
    while True:
        c=n&0x7f; n >>= 7
        if n: out.append(c|0x80)
        else:
            out.append(c); return bytes(out)

def parse_with_pos(data):
    i=0; out=[]
    while i<len(data):
        field_start=i
        key,i=read_varint(data,i); field=key>>3; wt=key&7
        val_start=i
        if wt==0:
            v,i=read_varint(data,i)
        elif wt==1:
            i+=8
        elif wt==2:
            l,i2=read_varint(data,i); len_start=i; i=i2; val_start=i; i+=l
        elif wt==5:
            i+=4
        else: raise Exception(wt)
        out.append((field,wt,field_start,val_start,i))
    return out

def parse(data):
    ret=[]
    for f,wt,fs,vs, end in parse_with_pos(data):
        if wt==2:
            l, i2=read_varint(data, fs+1) # assumes one byte key for our use? no
        ret.append((f,wt,data[vs:end]))
    return ret
# Better parse return field with value bytes for length delimiter only
def parse_values(data):
    i=0; out=[]
    while i<len(data):
        fs=i; key,i=read_varint(data,i); field=key>>3; wt=key&7
        if wt==0:
            vs=i; v,i=read_varint(data,i); out.append((field,wt,data[vs:i],fs,i,v))
        elif wt==1:
            vs=i; i+=8; out.append((field,wt,data[vs:i],fs,i,None))
        elif wt==2:
            l,i=read_varint(data,i); vs=i; i+=l; out.append((field,wt,data[vs:i],fs,i,None))
        elif wt==5:
            vs=i; i+=4; out.append((field,wt,data[vs:i],fs,i,None))
        else: raise Exception('wt')
    return out

fd_fields=parse_values(b)
# Build new FileDescriptorProto by replacing the Pod message's length-delimited field payload.
out=bytearray(); last=0; replaced=False
for f,wt,val,fs,end,iv in fd_fields:
    if f==4 and wt==2: # message_type
        name=None
        for mf,mwt,mval,mfs,mend,miv in parse_values(val):
            if mf==1 and mwt==2:
                name=mval.decode()
                break
        if name=='Pod':
            uid_field = bytes([0x12]) + enc_varint(15) + bytes([0x0a,0x03]) + b'uid' + bytes([0x18,0x03,0x20,0x01,0x28,0x09,0x52,0x03]) + b'uid'
            new_val=bytearray(); inserted=False; msg_fields=parse_values(val); last2=0
            for mf,mwt,mval,mfs,mend,miv in msg_fields:
                # insert after field named name (number 2), before container field
                field_name=None
                if mf==2 and mwt==2:
                    for ff,fw,fv,ffs,ffe,fiv in parse_values(mval):
                        if ff==1 and fw==2:
                            field_name=fv.decode()
                    if field_name=='container' and not inserted:
                        new_val.extend(val[last2:mfs]); new_val.extend(uid_field); last2=mfs; inserted=True
            new_val.extend(val[last2:])
            if not inserted: raise Exception('not inserted')
            out.extend(b[last:fs])
            # key for file message field 4 is 0x22
            out.extend(enc_varint((4<<3)|2)); out.extend(enc_varint(len(new_val))); out.extend(new_val)
            last=end; replaced=True
out.extend(b[last:])
print('replaced',replaced,'old',len(b),'new',len(out))
# format
nums=list(out)
lines=[]
for i in range(0,len(nums),12):
    chunk=', '.join(f'0x{x:02x}' for x in nums[i:i+12])
    lines.append('\t'+chunk+',')
new_literal='var file_tetragon_tetragon_proto_rawDesc = []byte{\n'+'\n'.join(lines)+'\n}'
src2=re.sub(r'var file_tetragon_tetragon_proto_rawDesc = \[\]byte\{.*?\n\}', new_literal, src, flags=re.S)
open('/tmp/tetragon.pb.go.new','w').write(src2)

cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/repro_pod_uid.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
if ! grep -q 'string uid = 3;' api/v1/tetragon/tetragon.proto; then
  echo 'missing uid field in Pod proto' >&2
  exit 1
fi
if ! grep -Eq 'Uid:[[:space:]]+string\(pod\.UID\)' pkg/process/podinfo.go; then
  echo 'pod UID is not populated from Kubernetes metadata' >&2
  exit 1
fi
if ! grep -q 'GetUid() string' api/v1/tetragon/tetragon.pb.go; then
  echo 'generated Pod type has no GetUid accessor' >&2
  exit 1
fi
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/repro_pod_uid.sh

def read(data,i):

 while True:
  c=data[i];i+=1;r|=(c&127)<<s
  if c<128:return r,i
  s+=7

def enc(n):
 out=[]
 while True:
  c=n&127;n>>=7
  if n:out.append(c|128)
  else:out.append(c);return bytes(out)

def parse(data, start=0, end=None):
 if end is None: end=len(data)
 i=start; out=[]
 while i<end:
  fs=i; key,i=read(data,i); f=key>>3; wt=key&7
  if wt==0:
   vs=i; v,i=read(data,i)
  elif wt==1: vs=i; i+=8; v=None
  elif wt==2:
   l,i=read(data,i); vs=i; i+=l; v=None
  elif wt==5: vs=i; i+=4; v=None
  else: raise Exception('wt',wt,'at',i,'key',key,'fs',fs,'context',data[max(0,fs-5):fs+10])
  out.append((f,wt,vs,i,v,fs))
 return out
for f,wt,vs,end,v,fs in parse(b):
 if f==4:
  val=b[vs:end]
  name=None
  for mf,mwt,mvs,mend,mv,mfs in parse(val):
   if mf==1:name=val[mvs:mend].decode()
  if name=='Pod':
   print('pod len',len(val), 'starts', fs)
   for mf,mwt,mvs,mend,mv,mfs in parse(val):
    if mf==2:
     fld=val[mvs:mend]
     d={}
     for ff,fw,fvs,fend,fv,ffs in parse(fld):
      if ff in (1,6,10): d[ff]=fld[fvs:fend].decode()
      elif ff in (3,4,5): d[ff]=fv
     print(d)

# validate raw descriptor parses and pod fields include uid
import re
body=re.search(r'file_tetragon_tetragon_proto_rawDesc = \[\]byte\{(.*?)\n\}', open('api/v1/tetragon/tetragon.pb.go').read(), re.S).group(1)
nums=[int(x,0) for x in re.findall(r'0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\b\d+\b', body)]
b=bytes(nums)
def read(data,i):
 s=0;r=0
 while True:
  c=data[i];i+=1;r|=(c&127)<<s
  if c<128:return r,i
  s+=7
def parse(data):
 i=0;out=[]
 while i<len(data):
  fs=i;key,i=read(data,i);f=key>>3;wt=key&7
  if wt==0: vs=i;v,i=read(data,i)
  elif wt==1: vs=i;i+=8;v=None
  elif wt==2: l,i=read(data,i);vs=i;i+=l;v=None
  elif wt==5: vs=i;i+=4;v=None
  else: raise Exception(wt,i)
  out.append((f,wt,data[vs:i],v))
 return out
for f,wt,val,_ in parse(b):
 if f==4:
  fields=parse(val); name=''
  for mf,mwt,mval,_ in fields:
   if mf==1: name=mval.decode()
  if name=='Pod':
   names=[]
   for mf,mwt,mval,_ in fields:
    if mf==2:
     d={}
     for ff,fw,fv,iv in parse(mval):
      if ff==1:d['name']=fv.decode()
      if ff==3:d['num']=iv
     names.append(d)
   print(names[:4])

package main

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"

    "github.com/cilium/tetragon/api/v1/tetragon"
)

func main() {
    p := &tetragon.Pod{Uid: "pod-uid-123"}
    b, err := json.Marshal(p)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    fmt.Println(p.GetUid())
    fmt.Println(string(b))
}

  2766	// ContainerChecker implements a checker struct to check a Container field
  2767	type ContainerChecker struct {
  2768	        Id              *stringmatcher.StringMatcher       `json:"id,omitempty"`
  2769	        Name            *stringmatcher.StringMatcher       `json:"name,omitempty"`
  2770	        Image           *ImageChecker                      `json:"image,omitempty"`
  2771	        StartTime       *timestampmatcher.TimestampMatcher `json:"startTime,omitempty"`
  2772	        Pid             *uint32                            `json:"pid,omitempty"`
  2773	        MaybeExecProbe  *bool                              `json:"maybeExecProbe,omitempty"`
  2774	        SecurityContext *SecurityContextChecker            `json:"securityContext,omitempty"`
  2775	}
  2776	
  2777	// NewContainerChecker creates a new ContainerChecker
  2778	func NewContainerChecker() *ContainerChecker {
  2779	        return &ContainerChecker{}
  2780	}
  2781	
  2782	// Get the type of the checker as a string
  2783	func (checker *ContainerChecker) GetCheckerType() string {
  2784	        return "ContainerChecker"
  2785	}
  2786	
  2787	// Check checks a Container field
  2788	func (checker *ContainerChecker) Check(event *tetragon.Container) error {
  2789	        if event == nil {
  2790	                return fmt.Errorf("%s: Container field is nil", CheckerLogPrefix(checker))
  2791	        }
  2792	
  2793	        fieldChecks := func() error {
  2794	                if checker.Id != nil {
  2795	                        if err := checker.Id.Match(event.Id); err != nil {
  2796	                                return fmt.Errorf("Id check failed: %w", err)
  2797	                        }
  2798	                }
  2799	                if checker.Name != nil {
  2800	                        if err := checker.Name.Match(event.Name); err != nil {
  2801	                                return fmt.Errorf("Name check failed: %w", err)
  2802	                        }
  2803	                }
  2804	                if checker.Image != nil {
  2805	                        if err := checker.Image.Check(event.Image); err != nil {
  2806	                                return fmt.Errorf("Image check failed: %w", err)
  2807	                        }
  2808	                }
  2809	                if checker.StartTime != nil {
  2810	                        if err := checker.StartTime.Match(event.StartTime); err != nil {
  2811	                                return fmt.Errorf("StartTime check failed: %w", err)
  2812	                        }
  2813	                }
  2814	                if checker.Pid != nil {
  2815	                        if event.Pid == nil {
  2816	                                return fmt.Errorf("Pid is nil and does not match expected value %v", *checker.Pid)
  2817	                        }
  2818	                        if *checker.Pid != event.Pid.Value {
  2819	                                return fmt.Errorf("Pid has value %v which does not match expected value %v", event.Pid.Value, *checker.Pid)
  2820	                        }
  2821	                }
  2822	                if checker.MaybeExecProbe != nil {
  2823	                        if *checker.MaybeExecProbe != event.MaybeExecProbe {
  2824	                                return fmt.Errorf("MaybeExecProbe has value %t which does not match expected value %t", event.MaybeExecProbe, *checker.MaybeExecProbe)
  2825	                        }
  2826	                }
  2827	                if checker.SecurityContext != nil {
  2828	                        if err := checker.SecurityContext.Check(event.SecurityContext); err != nil {
  2829	                                return fmt.Errorf("SecurityContext check failed: %w", err)
  2830	                        }
  2831	                }
  2832	                return nil
  2833	        }
  2834	        if err := fieldChecks(); err != nil {
  2835	                return fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", CheckerLogPrefix(checker), err)
  2836	        }
  2837	        return nil
  2838	}
  2839

  2882	//FromContainer populates the ContainerChecker using data from a Container field
  2883	func (checker *ContainerChecker) FromContainer(event *tetragon.Container) *ContainerChecker {
  2884	        if event == nil {
  2885	                return checker
  2886	        }
  2887	        checker.Id = stringmatcher.Full(event.Id)
  2888	        checker.Name = stringmatcher.Full(event.Name)
  2889	        if event.Image != nil {
  2890	                checker.Image = NewImageChecker().FromImage(event.Image)
  2891	        }
  2892	        // NB: We don't want to match timestamps for now
  2893	        checker.StartTime = nil
  2894	        if event.Pid != nil {
  2895	                val := event.Pid.Value
  2896	                checker.Pid = &val
  2897	        }
  2898	        {
  2899	                val := event.MaybeExecProbe
  2900	                checker.MaybeExecProbe = &val
  2901	        }
  2902	        if event.SecurityContext != nil {
  2903	                checker.SecurityContext = NewSecurityContextChecker().FromSecurityContext(event.SecurityContext)
  2904	        }
  2905	        return checker
  2906	}

package main

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"

    "github.com/cilium/tetragon/api/v1/tetragon"
)

func main() {
    p := &tetragon.Pod{Uid: "pod-uid-123"}
    b, err := json.Marshal(p)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    fmt.Println(p.GetUid())
    fmt.Println(string(b))
}
